ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: TWO DEMURRERS WERE FILED. THIS IS A COMBINED RULING THAT APPLIES TO BOTH. OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE A CONVERSION CLAIM. THE UCL CAUSE OF ACTION PLEADS ACTS OUTSIDE THE PREEMPTIVE SCOPE OF THE UTSA, THE PENAL CODE 502 IS ADEQUATELY PLED, AND THE CROSS-COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT THE EXECUTIVES ENGAGED IN UNREASONABLE AND WRONGFUL CONDUCT TO CREATE PERSONAL LIABILITY. THE COMMON LAW MISAPPROPRIATION AND INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BASED ON THE SAME NUCLEUS OF FACTS AS THE UTSA CAUSE OF ACTION. SBM, HOWEVER, HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION BASED ON THE THEFT OF ITS TANGIBLE PROPERTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT SBM SITE SERVICES, LLC'S Second Motion To Compel Further Discovery and scheduling a Further hearing is Granted in part and denied in part. No later than December 30, 2011 Able must provide all responsive information from January 1, 2008 to the present to special interrogatories 7, 9, 12-13, 15, 38, 41-51 and requests for production 3. Denied as to special interrogatory 19, which appears to be duplicative of special interrogatories 12 and 13 and further appears to seek information about documents Able has returned to SBM pursuant to the Colorado preliminary injunction order. The parties are directed to meet and confer and report on their progress in doing so at the hearing regarding special interrogatories 26-28, 32-36 and 39, which are overbroad but quite likely can be reasonably narrowed to seek information relevant to the issues, including damages, in the case. If the parties are unable to reach an agreement, they shall appear for further hearing on December 14, 2011 at 9:00 a.m.. If the parties reach an agreement they shall notify the court no later than 12/13/11 and submit a proposed order at the hearing on 12/14/11. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Kris Case.

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC., DBA ABLE BUILDING MAINTENANCE DBA ABLE SERVICES AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS MARK S. KELLY, RICHARD MULKERRINS AND MICHAEL MCGEE'S NOTICE AND MOTION TO STAY ACTION PURSUANT TO CCP 4l0.30(A) is Denied. Assuming without deciding that a party who initiates a lawsuit in a California court after a related lawsuit was underway in an assertedly suitable forum is not estopped from bringing a motion to stay the lawsuit it filed in favor of the prior action, plaintiff has not shown that this court is an inconvenient forum. Moveover, the public and private factors do not support a stay. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR 7077

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANTS MARK S. KELLY, RICHARD MULKERRINS, MICHAEL MCGEE AND CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT.
Two demurrers were filed. This is a combined ruling that applies to both. Overruled as to the first, second, and fourth causes of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the third and fifth causes of action, and ten days leave to amend to allege a conversion claim. The UCL cause of action pleads acts outside the preemptive scope of the UTSA, the Penal Code 502 is adequately pled, and the cross-complaint sufficiently alleges that the Executives engaged in unreasonable and wrongful conduct to create personal liability. The common law misappropriation and interference with prospective economic advantage causes of action are based on the same nucleus of facts as the UTSA cause of action. SBM, however, has leave to plead a cause of action for conversion based on the theft of its tangible property. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR 7077
